game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
Jazzpunk is a comedy adventure set in an alternate reality Cold War World, plagued with corporate espionage, CyberCrime, and sentient martinis. Gameplay is inspired by spoof comedy films and cartoons of yesteryear, with a focus on weird gadgets, exotic locales, and open-world style exploration. Jazzpunk is a comedy adventure set in an alternate reality Cold War World, plagued with corporate espionage, CyberCrime, and sentient martinis. Gameplay is inspired by spoof comedy films and cartoons of yesteryear, with a focus on weird gadgets, exotic locales, and open-world style exploration.

Key Features:

  • Stereographic Polygony Soundeo™, NOW IN COLOURVISON.
  • A cast of over 360,000 polygons, working in concert.
  • For the first time ever, the human voice captured and preserved in CryoSonic Stasis™, thawed before your very ears during NPC exchanges (voice acting).
  • Windows/Mac/Linux/Suppository
  • Half a dozen jokes.
  • Contains 5% of recommended daily intake of vitamin C++
  • Original soundtrack featuring exotic percussion and homemade analog electronic instruments.

Find out why PC Gamer says Jazzpunk is "easily one of the funniest games in years."
game_info.description: You're a secret agent in an absurd Cold War world filled with corporate espionage and bizarre gadgets. Explore a surreal office building, collect peculiar objects, interact with strange NPCs, and complete ridiculous missions. Your objective: gather enough intelligence items and find the secret exit to complete your mission. Watch out for security systems and roaming guards!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move agent around the office
Space: Interact with objects/NPCs
Z: Use equipped gadget
Shift: Sprint (moves faster)
Enter: Start game
Esc: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic movement and navigation around the game world. This test validates that the player can move in all four directions, collide properly with walls, and navigate through doorways. It ensures the core movement mechanics work correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The agent will systematically explore the map by moving in a pattern that covers all accessible areas. It will test collision detection by approaching walls from different angles and verify that doorways function correctly by moving through them.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The agent should be able to move smoothly in all directions without passing through walls. Position changes should be tracked correctly. The agent should successfully navigate through all doorways and explore the entire accessible map without getting stuck.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing the win condition by collecting intelligence items and finding the exit. This validates that the core gameplay loop works: collecting items increases score, and reaching the exit with sufficient items triggers the win state.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The agent will navigate to all intelligence item locations and interact with them to collect them. After collecting the required number of items, it will locate and move to the exit point to trigger the win condition.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The agent should successfully collect all intelligence items, with the score incrementing for each collection. Upon reaching the exit with enough items, the game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state. The test is successful when this win condition is achieved.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing interaction mechanics with NPCs and gadgets. This validates that the Space key properly triggers interactions with various game objects and that gadgets can be used with the Z key.
TEST_3.strategy_description: The agent will approach various NPCs and interactive objects, use the Space key to interact with them, and test gadget usage with the Z key. It will verify that interactions produce expected results like dialog display or item collection.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: All interactions should register correctly. NPCs should display dialog when approached and interacted with. Gadgets should activate and produce their intended effects. The inventory system should properly track collected items.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing guard AI and security systems. This validates that enemies patrol correctly, detect the player when in range, and trigger game over conditions when catching the player.
TEST_4.strategy_description: The agent will deliberately move into guard patrol paths and detection ranges to test the enemy AI behavior. It will verify that guards follow their patrol patterns and chase the player when detected.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Guards should patrol their assigned routes smoothly. When the player enters detection range, guards should pursue. If caught multiple times (depleting lives), the game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state.
